principles: One human task per canonical page; supporting concepts are natural language, not repeated keyword blocks.
Search and answer-engine visibility follows ordinary crawlability, useful text, evidence, and page experience.
No page claims scientific results, production operation, customers, adoption, or universal exactly-once execution.

/.primary_intent: understand Irrevon and the ambiguous external-effect problem

/platform/.primary_intent: inspect the current Irrevon engine and workbench capabilities

/how-it-works/.primary_intent: learn the reconciliation mechanism from intent through settlement

/demo/.primary_intent: inspect and reproduce the recorded synthetic response-loss demo

/benchmark/.primary_intent: understand the draft IrrevonBench methodology and falsification rule

/install/.primary_intent: install and run Irrevon from source

/docs/.primary_intent: navigate Irrevon guides and canonical technical references

/research/.primary_intent: read Irrevon research notes and evidence boundaries

/status/.primary_intent: verify what Irrevon has and has not implemented or evidenced

/security/.primary_intent: understand Irrevon security boundaries and report a vulnerability

/privacy/.primary_intent: understand runtime and website data handling

/contributing/.primary_intent: contribute to Irrevon under DCO and Apache-2.0

/roadmap/.primary_intent: understand Irrevon release gates and remaining work

patterns.primary_intent: read a canonical Irrevon design, governance, contract, or operations reference
complete a specific Irrevon engineering or conceptual task
understand a specific Irrevon research claim, source, or limitation

noindex./docs/search/: internal search-result surface; canonical documentation remains crawlable
